title: Breitmann in Kansas
content:
آ آ آ آ Vonce oopon a dimes, goot vhile afder der var vas ofer, der Herr Breitmann vent oud Vest, drafellinï؟½ apout like efery dings ï؟½circuivit terram et perambulavit eam,ï؟½ ash der Teufel said ven dey ask him: ï؟½How vash you und how you has peen?ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Von efenings he vas drafel mit some ladies und shendlemans, und he shtaid incognitus. Und dey singed songs, dill py und py one of de ladies say: ï؟½Ish any podies here ash know de crate pallad of Hans Breitmannï؟½s Barty?ï؟½ Den Hans say: ï؟½Ecce Gallus! I am dat rooster!ï؟½ Den der Hans dook a trink und a let-bencil und a biece of baper, und goes indo himself a little dimes und den coomes out again mit dis boem:

آ آ آ آ Hans Breitmann vent to Kansas:
آ آ آ آ He drafel fast und far;
آ آ آ آ He rided shoost drei dousand miles
آ آ آ آ All in von rail-roat car.
آ آ آ آ He knowed foost rate how far he goed
آ آ آ آ He gounted all de vile,
آ آ آ آ Dere vash shoost one bottle of champagne,
آ آ آ آ Dat bopped at efery mile.

آ آ آ آ Hans Breitmann vent to Kansas;
آ آ آ آ I dell you vot, my poy,
آ آ آ آ You bet dey hat a pully dimes
آ آ آ آ In crossinï؟½ Illinoy.
آ آ آ آ Dey speaked deir speaks to all de folk
آ آ آ آ A shtandinï؟½ in de car;
آ آ آ آ Den ask dem in to dake a trink,
آ آ آ آ Und corned em gans und gar.

آ آ آ آ Hans Breitmann vent to Kansas;
آ آ آ آ By shings! dey did it prown.
آ آ آ آ When he got into Leafenvort,
آ آ آ آ He found himself in town.
آ آ آ آ Dey dined him at de Blanterï؟½s House,
آ آ آ آ More goot as man could dink;
آ آ آ آ Mit efery dings on eartï؟½ to eat,
آ آ آ آ Und dwice as mooch to trink.

آ آ آ آ Hans Breitmann vent to Kansas;
آ آ آ آ He vent it on de loud.
آ آ آ آ At Ellsvort, in de prairie land,
آ آ آ آ He foundt a pully crowd.
آ آ آ آ He looked for bleedinï؟½ Kansas,
آ آ آ آ But datï؟½s ï؟½blayed out,ï؟½ dey say;
آ آ آ آ De vhiskey kegï؟½s de only ding
آ آ آ آ Datï؟½s bleedinï؟½ dere to-day.

آ آ آ آ Hans Breitmann vent to Kansas,
آ آ آ آ To see vot he could hear.
آ آ آ آ He foundt soom Deutschers dat exisdt
آ آ آ آ Py makinï؟½ lager beer.
آ آ آ آ Says he: ï؟½Wie gehts du Alt Gesell?ï؟½
آ آ آ آ But nodings could be heard;
آ آ آ آ Deyï؟½d growed so fat in Kansas
آ آ آ آ Dat dey couldnï؟½t speak a vord.

آ آ آ آ Hans Breitmann vent to Kansas;
آ آ آ آ Py shings! I dell you vot,
آ آ آ آ Von day he met a crisly bear
آ آ آ آ Dat rooshed him down, bei Gott!
آ آ آ آ Boot der Breitmann took und bind der bear
آ آ آ آ Und bleased him fery much
آ آ آ آ For efery vordt der crisly growled
آ آ آ آ Vas goot Bavarian Dutch!

آ آ آ آ Hans Breitmann vent to Kansas!
آ آ آ آ By donder dat is so!
آ آ آ آ He ridet oout upon de blains
آ آ آ آ To shase de boofalo.
آ آ آ آ He fired his rifle at de bools,
آ آ آ آ Und gallop droo de shmoke,
آ آ آ آ Und shoomp de canyons shoost as if
آ آ آ آ Der teufel vas a choke!

آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s hey de trail to Santa Fï؟½;
آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s ho! agross de plain;
آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s lope along de Denver road,
آ آ آ آ Until ve toorn again.
آ آ آ آ Und de railroad drafel after us
آ آ آ آ Apout as quick as ve;
آ آ آ آ Dis Kansas ish de fastest land
آ آ آ آ Ash efer I did see.

آ آ آ آ Hans Breitmann vent to Kansas;
آ آ آ آ He have a pully dime;
آ آ آ آ But ï؟½twas in old Missouri
آ آ آ آ Dat dey rooshed him up subline.
آ آ آ آ Dey took him to der Bilot Nob,
آ آ آ آ Und all der nobs around;
آ آ آ آ Dey shpreed him und dey teaï؟½d him
آ آ آ آ Dill dey roon him to de ground.

آ آ آ آ Hans Breitmann vent to Kansas,
آ آ آ آ Und made his carpine pop!
آ آ آ آ Ven he shooted at a drifer man
آ آ آ آ To make de wagon shdop.
آ آ آ آ A noble Tribune shendleman
آ آ آ آ Shoost dodged dat pulletï؟½s bore,
آ آ آ آ Und de driver shwore dat soosh a crowd
آ آ آ آ He nefer druv pefore.

آ آ آ آ Hans Breitmann vent to Kansas;
آ آ آ آ Droo all dis earthly land,
آ آ آ آ A vorkinï؟½ out lifeï؟½s mission here
آ آ آ آ Soobyectifly und grand.
آ آ آ آ Some beoplesh runs de beautiful,
آ آ آ آ Some vorks philosophie;
آ آ آ آ Der Breitmann solfe de infinide
آ آ آ آ Ash von eternal shpree!
meaning:
"Breitmann in Kansas" is a quintessential example of Charles G. Leland's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
